conversation.content:
Reconcile the following columns. Document has 18 pages. Sources are anonymous (A and B).

COLUMNS:

---
Column 1: Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | Synchronous
Definition: What indicates whether the results of the clinical trial were reported based on prognostic groups with synchronous metastases, where synchronous metastases refers to metastases present at the time of diagnosis of prostate cancer. with a response of either "yes" (Y) or "no" (N)? Answer Yes or No;
  A: value="Y" | page=5 | modality=table | reasoning="The paper reports results for 'de novo mHSPC' (synchronous) in Table 1 and Appendix Fig A1."
  B: value="Y" | page=2 | modality=text | reasoning="Page 2 states: 'In ARASENS, the treatment effect of darolutamide on OS was favorable in patients with de novo (HR, 0.71; 95% CI, 0.59 to 0.85) and recurrent (HR, 0.61; 95% CI, 0.35 to 1.05) metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer.' De novo refers to synchronous disease."

---
Column 2: Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | Metachronous
Definition: What indicates whether the results of the clinical trial were reported based on prognostic groups of patients with metachronous diseases (diseases that occur at different times). with a response of either "yes" (Y) or "no" (N)? Answer Yes or No
  A: value="Y" | page=5 | modality=table | reasoning="The paper reports results for 'Recurrent mHSPC' (metachronous) in Table 1 and Appendix Fig A1."
  B: value="Y" | page=2 | modality=text | reasoning="Page 2 states: 'In ARASENS, the treatment effect of darolutamide on OS was favorable in patients with de novo (HR, 0.71; 95% CI, 0.59 to 0.85) and recurrent (HR, 0.61; 95% CI, 0.35 to 1.05) metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer.' Recurrent refers to metachronous disease."

---
Column 3: Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | High volume
Definition: What indicates whether the results of the clinical trial were reported based on prognostic groups with high volume/burden of metastases, with a response of either "yes" (Y) or "no" (N)? Answer Yes or No
  A: value="Y" | page=5 | modality=table | reasoning="The abstract and Table 1 explicitly report results for 'high-volume disease'."
  B: value="Y" | page=1 | modality=text | reasoning="Page 1 states: 'Of 1,305 patients, 1,005 (77%) had high-volume disease... Darolutamide increased overall survival (OS) versus placebo in patients with high-volume (hazard ratio [HR], 0.69; 95% CI, 0.57 to 0.82)'. Page 2 defines high-volume disease."

---
Column 4: Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | Low volume
Definition: What indicates whether the results were reported based on prognostic groups having a low volume of metastasis, with a yes or no response (Y/N)? Answer Yes or No
  A: value="Y" | page=5 | modality=table | reasoning="The abstract and Table 1 explicitly report results for 'low-volume disease'."
  B: value="Y" | page=1 | modality=text | reasoning="Page 1 states: '...and in the smaller low-volume subgroup, the results were also suggestive of survival benefit (HR, 0.68; 95% CI, 0.41 to 1.13).' Page 2 defines low-volume disease as not meeting high-volume criteria."

---
Column 5: Adverse Events - N (%) | All-Cause Grade 3 or Higher | Treatment
Definition: What the number and percentage of patients experiencing adverse events of all causes with a grade of 3 or higher in the treatment arm of the trial? If reported, include count and/or percentage. If multiple relevant arms are reported, list each arm separately. Use 'Not reported' if missing.
  A: value="458 (70.2%)" | page=9 | modality=table | reasoning="From Table 3, 'All Patients' column, Darolutamide (n=652): Grade 3 (248), Grade 4 (183), Grade 5 (27). Sum = 458. Percentage = 458/652 = 70.24%."
  B: value="431 (66.1%)" | page=9 | modality=table | reasoning="From Page 9, Table 3, 'All Patients' column, Darolutamide arm (n=652): Grade 3 AEs were 248 (38.0%) and Grade 4 AEs were 183 (28.1%). Summing these gives 431 (66.1%)."

---
Column 6: Adverse Events - N (%) | All-Cause Grade 3 or Higher | Control
Definition: What number and percentage of patients experiencing all-cause adverse events with a grade of 3 or higher in the control arm of the trial? If reported, include count and/or percentage. If multiple relevant arms are reported, list each arm separately. Use 'Not reported' if missing.
  A: value="439 (67.5%)" | page=9 | modality=table | reasoning="From Table 3, 'All Patients' column, Placebo (n=650): Grade 3 (232), Grade 4 (181), Grade 5 (26). Sum = 439. Percentage = 439/650 = 67.54%."
  B: value="413 (63.5%)" | page=9 | modality=table | reasoning="From Page 9, Table 3, 'All Patients' column, Placebo arm (n=650): Grade 3 AEs were 232 (35.7%) and Grade 4 AEs were 181 (27.8%). Summing these gives 413 (63.5%)."

---
Column 7: Adverse Events - N (%) | Treatment-related Grade 3 or Higher | Treatment
Definition: What number and percentage of patients experiencing treatment-related adverse events with a grade of 3 or higher in the treatment arm of the trial? If reported, include count and/or percentage. If multiple relevant arms are reported, list each arm separately. Use 'Not reported' if missing.
  A: value="Not reported" | page=None | modality=text | reasoning="Table 3 reports 'Worst grade' for 'Any AE' but does not specify 'treatment-related' AEs by grade. The text mentions 'incidences of most AEs commonly associated with androgen receptor pathway inhibitors' but not a total for treatment-related Grade 3 or higher."
  B: value="Not reported" | page=None | modality=text | reasoning="The document reports 'Treatment-Emergent AEs' (all-cause) in Table 3 on Page 9, but does not provide a breakdown for 'treatment-related' adverse events of Grade 3 or higher. Specific searches for this information did not yield results."

Column 8: Adverse Events - N (%) | Treatment-related Grade 3 or Higher | Control
Definition: What the number and percentage of patients experiencing adverse events that are related to the treatment and have a severity grade of 3 or higher in the control arm of the clinical trial? If reported, include count and/or percentage. If multiple relevant arms are reported, list each arm separately. Use 'Not reported' if missing.

Column 9: Adverse Events - N (%) | Treatment-related Grade 5 | Treatment
Definition: What the number and percentage of patients showing adverse events that are treatment-related and have a grade of 5 in the treatment arm of the trial? Definition: What the number and percentage of patients experiencing treatment-related adverse events with a grade of 5 in the control arm of the clinical trial? Abbreviations: AE, adverse event; EAIR, exposure-adjusted incidence rate.

aDisease volume defined by CHAARTED criteria: presence of visceral metastases or ≥ 4 bone metastases with ≥ 1 beyond vertebral bodies and pelvis (Sweeney et al).¹
bDisease risk defined by LATITUDE criteria (two of the following three factors): Gleason score of ≥ 8, the presence of ≥ 3 bone lesions, or the presence of measurable visceral metastasis (Fizazi et al).²
cThree patients who underwent random assignment never received the assigned trial treatment; all three patients were in the placebo group. One patient who was assigned to the placebo group but received darolutamide was included in the darolutamide group of the safety analysis set.
dSpecific grade 5 events are provided in the supplementary appendix of Smith et al.⁹
eNeutropenia includes preferred terms of leukopenia, neutropenia, decreased neutrophil count, and decreased white blood cell count. Exposure adjustment is not applicable, as the majority of AEs occurred during concomitant docetaxel treatment.



9







Darolutamide, ADT, and Docetaxel in mHSPC Volume/Risk Subgroups

submitted: Adverse Events - N (%) | All-Cause Grade 3 or Higher | Treatment
Adverse Events - N (%) | All-Cause Grade 3 or Higher | Control

Adverse Events - N (%) | All-Cause Grade.reasoning: From Table 3 on page 9, for the Darolutamide arm (n=652), Grade 3 AEs were 248, Grade 4 AEs were 183, and Grade 5 AEs were 27. Summing these gives 248 + 183 + 27 = 458. The percentage is (458/652)*100 = 70.245%, which rounds to 70.2%. Source A correctly includes Grade 5 events, while Source B does not.

From Table 3 on page 9, for the Placebo arm (n=650), Grade 3 AEs were 232, Grade 4 AEs were 181, and Grade 5 AEs were 26. Summing these gives 232 + 181 + 26 = 439. The percentage is (439/650)*100 = 67.538%, which rounds to 67.5%. Source A correctly includes Grade 5 events, while Source B does not.
